what: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
text: Performs Bayesian Frequentist two-sample t-test using RTMB.

title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
code: rtmb_ttest(   x,   y = NULL,   data = NULL,   r = 0.707,   paired = FALSE,   ID = NULL,   y_range = NULL,   prior = prior_flat(),   init = NULL,   fixed = NULL,   var.equal = TRUE,   missing = c("listwise", "fiml"),   WAIC = FALSE,   ... )

title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
text: x Numeric vector responses group 1, formula (e.g., `y ~ group`), column name (unquoted) `data` provided. y Numeric vector responses group 2, column name (unquoted) `data` provided. Required `x` formula. data Data frame containing variables. r Numeric; Cauchy prior scale effect size (delta). Default 0.707. paired Logical; whether perform paired t-test. ID Character; name ID variable paired t-tests (required formula input paired = TRUE). y_range Theoretical minimum maximum values response variable vector c(min, max). Required using weakly informative priors. prior object class `"rtmb_prior"`. Use `prior_flat()` prior, `prior_normal()` default normal/exponential priors, `prior_jzs()` JZS t-test priors, `prior_weak()` weakly informative Bayesian inference. Default `prior_flat()`. init List initial values. fixed Optional named list fixed values specific parameters. var.equal Logical; whether assume equal variances. Default TRUE. missing Missing value handling strategy: "listwise". WAIC Logical; TRUE, add pointwise `log_lik` generate block WAIC. ... Reserved; unused arguments rejected.

title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
text: `RTMB_Model` object.

title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
text: classic inference, heteroscedastic two-sample t-tests use RTMB Satterthwaite machinery `optimize(marginal = ..., df_method = "satterthwaite")`. result model-based reproducible printed model code. corresponds Welch-type unequal-variance t-test, degrees freedom computed package's internal Satterthwaite procedure rather separate closed-form formula. `prior_jzs()` combined `var.equal = FALSE`, BayesRTMB uses Welch-style JZS extension: effect size `delta` explicit parameter Cauchy prior, group mean difference scaled root-mean-square two group standard deviations.

title: RTMB-based Bayesian two-sample t-test wrapper function — rtmb_ttest
code: # Simulate two-sample data with a true effect size   set.seed(123)   y1 <- rnorm(30, mean = 0.5, sd = 1)   y2 <- rnorm(30, mean = 0.0, sd = 1)    # Fit the Bayesian two-sample t-test model   # r = 0.707 is the standard scale for the Cauchy prior on the effect size   fit_ttest <- rtmb_ttest(y1, y2, prior = prior_jzs(r = 0.707)) #> Pre-checking model code... #> Checking RTMB setup...    # \donttest{   # MCMC sampling (chains and iterations reduced for faster execution)   mcmc_ttest <- fit_ttest$sample(sampling = 500, warmup = 500, chains = 2) #> Starting sequential sampling (chains = 2)... #> chain 1 started... #> chain 1: iter 200/1000 (20%) warmup #> chain 1: iter 400/1000 (40%) warmup #> chain 1: iter 600/1000 (60%) sampling #> chain 1: iter 800/1000 (80%) sampling #> chain 1: iter 1000/1000 (100%) sampling #> chain 1 done (100%) #> chain 2 started... #> chain 2: iter 200/1000 (20%) warmup #> chain 2: iter 400/1000 (40%) warmup #> chain 2: iter 600/1000 (60%) sampling #> chain 2: iter 800/1000 (80%) sampling #> chain 2: iter 1000/1000 (100%) sampling #> chain 2 done (100%) #> sampling: 100% #> Calculating transformed parameters... #> transformed parameters: 20% #> transformed parameters: 40% #> transformed parameters: 60% #> transformed parameters: 80% #> transformed parameters: 100%   mcmc_ttest$summary() #>   variable    mean    sd     map    q2.5   q97.5  ess_bulk  ess_tail  rhat  #> lp          -81.01  1.27  -80.20  -84.28  -79.67       521       586  1.00  #> diff          0.24  0.23    0.16   -0.21    0.71       759       463  1.00  #> delta         0.26  0.25    0.17   -0.22    0.78       723       445  1.00  #> total_mean    0.32  0.11    0.32    0.10    0.54       854       618  1.00  #> sd            0.93  0.08    0.89    0.78    1.10       952       779  1.00  #> mean0         0.44  0.16    0.47    0.12    0.79       802       602  1.00  #> mean1         0.20  0.16    0.22   -0.13    0.50       748       604  1.00    # }

what: Common Features and Arguments of RTMB Wrapper Functions — rtmb_wrappers
title: Common Features and Arguments of RTMB Wrapper Functions — rtmb_wrappers
text: RTMB wrapper functions (`rtmb_lm`, `rtmb_glm`, `rtmb_glmer`, `rtmb_fa`, etc.) share unified interface designed make Bayesian Frequentist inference accessible familiar R formulas standard model specifications.

title: Common Features and Arguments of RTMB Wrapper Functions — rtmb_wrappers
text: wrapper functions package built upon core engine. ensures regardless model type, workflow estimation, summary, expansion remains consistent. 1. Unified Inference Methods: Every model object returned wrapper function provides following methods: $classic(): Performs standard frequentist estimation (MLE/REML) using `prior_flat()`. Supports robust standard errors Satterthwaite/-Within degrees freedom approximations. $optimize(): Performs Maximum Posteriori (MAP) estimation (Empirical Bayes). $sample(): Performs MCMC sampling (NUTS via tmbstan) full Bayesian inference. $variational(): Performs Variational Inference (ADVI) fast posterior approximation. 2. Prior API Regularization: can specify prior distribution using `prior` argument: prior_flat(): additional prior (default). Required classic() inference. prior_normal(): Manual normal/exponential priors parameters. prior_weak(): Weakly informative priors based y_range. prior_rhs(): Regularized Horseshoe prior continuous shrinkage. prior_ssp(): Spike--Slab prior sparse variable selection. Note: using `prior_weak()`, `prior_rhs()`, `prior_ssp()`, must specify y_range = c(min, max) let model set appropriate global scales priors. 3. Model Comparison Bayes Factors: Model comparison performed exclusively via Bridge Sampling. compare full model nested null model, use $bayes_factor(fixed = list(...)) method MCMC fit object. example, fit$bayes_factor(fixed = list(b = 0)) tests hypothesis fixed effect b exactly zero. 4. Standard Errors Degrees Freedom: $classic() $optimize() methods provide advanced summary options: se_method: Calculate `"robust"` (Huber-White) `"cluster-robust"` standard errors providing cluster variable. df_method: Use `"satterthwaite"` mixed models `"bw"` (-Within) multilevel correlation models obtain accurate p-values confidence intervals. 5. Fixed parameters: Use fixed = list(...) model construction fix model parameters specified values. example, fixed = list(delta = 0) fixes parameter delta zero. syntax used $bayes_factor() define restricted model.

title: BayesRTMB 0.2.2
text: Added response-time distributions model code: exp_mod_normal_lpdf() diffusion_lpdf(), sampling syntax support via exp_mod_normal(...) obs(RT, Choice) ~ diffusion(...). Added obs(...) sampling syntax multivariate observed values left side ~. Improved setup helper capture functions referenced setup reliably available building models running parallel workers. Improved factor-analysis AD robustness rotation output naming.

title: BayesRTMB 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-23 Added upgrade_fit() rebuild saved MCMC, VB, MAP, classic fit objects currently loaded class definitions, optionally upgrading embedded model objects well. Improved rtmb_vector() rtmb_array() tape construction time automatically reusing AD seed model parameters available. Made log_sum_exp(), softmax(), log_softmax() work reliably RTMB automatic-differentiation values, including baseline-category patterns softmax(c(0, eta)) inside rtmb_code(). Improved wrapper-generated model code use AD-compatible rtmb_vector() rtmb_array() containers loop-filled generated quantities generated likelihood contributions needed. Improved report() handling transformed generated quantities, including namespaced BayesRTMB::report() calls wrapper-generated print_code() output. Changed VB point estimates use best variational estimate default, aligning EAP(), MAP(), rotation references selected best ELBO run still allowing explicit chains best_chains selection. Made EAP() MAP() drop list wrapper default single parameter requested, matching behavior estimate(). Added optional taped evaluation transformed parameters generated quantities, automatic fallback R evaluation taping possible. Improved parallel worker robustness reducing exported globals preserving wrapper setup environments needed generated model code. Improved MCMC runtime behavior caching metric calculations, speeding retained draw conversion, refining progress checks. Changed bootstrap progress reporting use percentage-style progress output, consistent long-running workflows. Added diagnostic recommendations help interpret common fitting warnings. Improved matrix-valued Gaussian process log-density evaluation. Updated MDU defaults internals, including Euclidean distance default MDU distance explicit use namespace-qualified factor rotations.

title: BayesRTMB 0.2.0
text: Reworked NUTS sampling internals Stan-style multinomial tree expansion, warmup diagnostics, Stan-window metric adaptation, support diagonal, dense, hybrid, automatic metric selection. Improved MCMC diagnostics reporting divergence counts percentages, metric auto-selection details, warmup summaries, metric condition numbers, positive-definite fallback counts. Added configurable progress output MCMC VB workflows, including streamed message-style progress parallel workers percentage reporting. Added delta-method standard errors confidence intervals conditional_effects() simple_effects() optimized classic fits; simple_effects() classic fits now also reports df, t value, Pr. Added sd_slice sd_multiplier controls conditional simple effects, including automatic SD slicing moderators many observed values. Added rhat_summary() MCMC fits, returning numeric R-hat vector compact printed summary. Expanded data-reshaping helpers: to_long() now supports multiple value columns, list-based column groups, preserves input row order default still allowing sorted output sort = TRUE. Added AD-compatible helper constructors rtmb_vector() rtmb_array() model code needs mutable RTMB-compatible containers. Improved RTMB model setup error messages common AD NA/NaN failures. Improved wrapper behavior, including rtmb_glmer(cwc = list(ID, "")), hierarchical lambda rtmb_mdu(), stronger prior validation, robust handling non-finite VB optimization attempts. Improved MDU plotting initialization, including principal-axis reference rotation clearer radius display controls. Fixed several model-specific issues, including AD-compatible negative-binomial log densities unequal-variance JZS t-test examples.

title: BayesRTMB 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-01 Addressed CRAN resubmission feedback quoting ‘RTMB’ package title, adding method references DESCRIPTION, replacing \dontrun{} examples \donttest{} appropriate. Trimmed long-running examples CRAN checks retaining representative MCMC examples correlation, t-test, mixed-model workflows. Updated IRT post-estimation examples use ordered response data explicitly. Fixed AD-compatible negative-binomial log-density issue. Corrected unequal-variance JZS t-test example documentation.
